Title: Soybean gene co-expression network analysis identifies two co-regulated gene modules associated with nodule formation and development

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: One-carbon cofactors supporting cell division and biosynthesis of nucleic acids and amino acids
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in developing nodules, roots, leaf buds, and flowers
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in developing nodules, roots, leaf buds, and flowers
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss-of-function mutations reduced nodule number and nodule size and were associated with reduced nitrogen fixation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Glyma.19G121600 encodes a dihydrofolate reductase involved in folate metabolism. 2. Three TILLING mutants showed significantly fewer nodules than wild type. 3. Mutants also showed reduced nodule size and brown nodules indicative of reduced nitrogen fixation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: TILLING mutant analysis; sequencing confirmation of mutant alleles; Bradyrhizobium diazoefficiens inoculation; nodule counting; nodule size measurement; nodule cross-sections/light microscopy
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Inoculation with Bradyrhizobium diazoefficiens strain USDA110; evaluation 22 days after inoculation
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (138 datasets used for WGCNA; no accession reported)
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate target to improve nodule formation and nitrogen fixation by maintaining folate metabolism during nodulation; requires caution because folate metabolism is central to growth and may have pleiotropic effects
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely broad pleiotropic effects on cell division and primary metabolism due to central role of folates
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper frames the phenotype through folate metabolism affecting symbiosis-related processes such as purine biosynthesis, DNA methylation, endoreduplication, nitrogen translocation, and hormone biosynthesis; direct folate metabolite measurements were not performed

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Stearic acid to oleic acid desaturation step
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces a double bond into stearoyl-ACP to form oleoyl-ACP/oleic acid
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Membrane lipid and fatty acid components implicated in nodule formation and development
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Specifically highly expressed in nodules; reported as the only soybean stearoyl-ACP desaturase gene expressed in nodules
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Mutations reduced nodule number and were associated with reduced nitrogen fixation; some mutant lines also had smaller nodules
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Glyma.14G121400 encodes a stearoyl-ACP desaturase that converts stearic acid to oleic acid. 2. Five TILLING mutants formed significantly fewer nodules than wild type. 3. Mutant nodules were brown, indicating reduced nitrogen fixation, and two lines also showed reduced nodule size.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: TILLING mutant analysis; sequencing confirmation of mutant alleles; Bradyrhizobium diazoefficiens inoculation; nodule counting; nodule size measurement; nodule cross-sections/light microscopy
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Inoculation with Bradyrhizobium diazoefficiens strain USDA110; evaluation 22 days after inoculation
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (138 datasets used for WGCNA; no accession reported)
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point for unsaturated fatty acid levels in nodules not fully established in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for improving nodule development and nitrogen fixation through tuning unsaturated fatty acid metabolism; should be optimized carefully because fatty acid pathways are interconnected
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Mutations may broadly alter lipid metabolism and multiple nodule phenotypic and physiological traits
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Soybean has five stearoyl-ACP desaturase genes, but only Glyma.14G121400 is reported as expressed in nodules in the cited transcriptome dataset; exact altered lipid metabolites were not profiled in this study
